Requirement: K1 response must be externally derived and nonzero on at least part of the coordinate-native target grid
Reason: Multiplicative K2 cannot separate from no-memory if K1 is identically zero
AllowedApproach: derive K1 from public reconstruction-family mean response or likelihood-native baseline
ForbiddenApproach: fit K1 to make K2 pass the current source-split residuals
NextAction: Define a nonzero K1 response target before rerunning distinct K2 scoring

Requirement: K1 amplitude must not be tuned to rescue K2 on the current scorecard
AllowedApproach: freeze amplitude from independent public reconstruction or predeclared normalization
ForbiddenApproach: tune amplitude/rho after seeing K2 residuals
NextAction: Document amplitude source before any K2 scorecard

Requirement: K1 must live on the same x grid and target rows as the source-split benchmark
AllowedApproach: export K1 rows keyed by TargetID and GridIndex
ForbiddenApproach: use different redshift grid or interpolate after scoring

Requirement: K1 must be scored under the same covariance policy as K2 and nulls
AllowedApproach: use declared shrinkage covariance or future public full covariance
ForbiddenApproach: compare K1 and K2 under different covariance matrices
NextAction: Replace shrinkage policy with public full covariance when available

Requirement: No-memory K1 must remain a fair null comparator
Reason: Prevents turning baseline into hidden signal
AllowedApproach: report K1/no-memory and K2 side by side
ForbiddenApproach: hide K1 because K2 is weak
NextAction: Keep K1 rows in all source-split scorecards
